edges.description: Failure to mature and incorporate COX2 yields a catalytically deficient Complex IV.
edges.source: COX20 Loss and Defective COX2 Maturation
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Bioenergetic failure in neural and skeletal-muscle tissue produces early hypotonia.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Bioenergetic failure in the cerebellum produces progressive ataxia.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Cerebellar and extrapyramidal dysfunction downstream of Complex IV deficiency produces dysarthric speech.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy failure in motor-control circuits contributes to dystonia in the COX20 movement-disorder phenotype.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Sensory-predominant axonal neuropathy downstream of Complex IV deficiency produces areflexia.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy deficit in long sensory axons produces a sensory-predominant axonal neuropathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

nodes.description: Areflexia, reflecting the associated sensory-predominant peripheral neuropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Novel pathogenic COX20 variants causing dysarthria, ataxia, and sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: We report four subjects with features that include childhood hypotonia, areflexia, ataxia, dysarthria, dystonia, and sensory neuropathy

nodes.description: Progressive cerebellar ataxia, a cardinal feature of COX20-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the FAM36A gene, the human ortholog of COX20, impairs cytochrome c oxidase assembly and is associated with ataxia and muscle hypotonia.
evidence.snippet: we describe a homozygous missense mutation in FAM36A from a patient who displays ataxia and muscle hypotonia

evidence.reference_title: Novel pathogenic COX20 variants causing dysarthria, ataxia, and sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: We report four subjects with features that include childhood hypotonia, areflexia, ataxia, dysarthria, dystonia, and sensory neuropathy

nodes.description: Biallelic COX20 variants cause loss of the inner-membrane COX2 chaperone. COX20 binds newly synthesized COX2, stabilizes it, and presents it to the SCO1/SCO2 copper-metallation module; in its absence COX2 is unstable and Complex IV stalls at an early subassembly that contains COX1 (and COX4) but is depleted of COX2, yielding isolated Complex IV biogenesis failure.
nodes.id: COX20 Loss and Defective COX2 Maturation

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the FAM36A gene, the human ortholog of COX20, impairs cytochrome c oxidase assembly and is associated with ataxia and muscle hypotonia.
evidence.snippet: Cells affected by the mutation accumulate subassemblies of complex IV that contain COX1 but are almost devoid of COX2 protein

evidence.reference_title: Human COX20 cooperates with SCO1 and SCO2 to mature COX2 and promote the assembly of cytochrome c oxidase.
evidence.snippet: These cell lines exhibit a severe, isolated CIV deficiency due to instability of COX2, a mitochondrion-encoded CIV subunit

evidence.reference_title: Human COX20 cooperates with SCO1 and SCO2 to mature COX2 and promote the assembly of cytochrome c oxidase.
evidence.snippet: We propose that COX20 acts as a chaperone in the early steps of COX2 maturation, stabilizing the newly synthesized protein and presenting COX2 to its metallochaperone module

nodes.description: Dysarthric speech, part of the COX20-related cerebellar/extrapyramidal syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Novel pathogenic COX20 variants causing dysarthria, ataxia, and sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: We report four subjects with features that include childhood hypotonia, areflexia, ataxia, dysarthria, dystonia, and sensory neuropathy

nodes.description: Dystonia, reported as part of the COX20-related movement disorder.

evidence.reference_title: Novel pathogenic COX20 variants causing dysarthria, ataxia, and sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: We report four subjects with features that include childhood hypotonia, areflexia, ataxia, dysarthria, dystonia, and sensory neuropathy

nodes.description: Early/childhood hypotonia, often the presenting feature of COX20-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the FAM36A gene, the human ortholog of COX20, impairs cytochrome c oxidase assembly and is associated with ataxia and muscle hypotonia.
evidence.snippet: we describe a homozygous missense mutation in FAM36A from a patient who displays ataxia and muscle hypotonia

nodes.description: Deficient Complex IV blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis and preferentially injuring high-energy neural tissue (cerebellum, peripheral sensory axons, skeletal muscle).
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the FAM36A gene, the human ortholog of COX20, impairs cytochrome c oxidase assembly and is associated with ataxia and muscle hypotonia.
evidence.snippet: leads to a reduced activity and lower levels of complex IV and its protein subunits

nodes.description: Sensory-predominant axonal neuropathy, a recurrent feature of COX20-related disease and the dominant presentation in some families.

evidence.reference_title: Novel pathogenic COX20 variants causing dysarthria, ataxia, and sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: We report four subjects with features that include childhood hypotonia, areflexia, ataxia, dysarthria, dystonia, and sensory neuropathy

evidence.reference_title: Observation of novel COX20 mutations related to autosomal recessive axonal neuropathy and static encephalopathy.
evidence.snippet: we describe two patients from a non-consanguineous family exhibiting autosomal recessive sensory-dominant axonal neuropathy and static encephalopathy
